problem:
Here are two functions: $$\begin{array}{ccc}
f(x) & = & 3x^2-2x+ 4\\
g(x) & = & x^2-kx-6
\end{array}$$ If $f(10) - g(10) = 10,$ what is the value of $k?$

classified_questions.question: What does the expression $f(10)$ represent? Can you explain its meaning in the context of the function $f(x)$?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Similarly, what does the expression $g(10)$ represent? Can you explain its meaning in the context of the function $g(x)$?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: If we substitute $x=10$ into the functions $f(x)$ and $g(x)$, what do we get? Can you simplify the expressions?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Given that $f(10) - g(10) = 10$, what can we infer about the values of $f(10)$ and $g(10)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Using the information obtained from the previous question, what equation can we set up to find the value of $k$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we simplify the equation obtained in the previous question? What steps can we take to isolate the variable $k$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: After simplifying and solving the equation, what is the final value of $k$?
classified_questions.label: b
